items.headline: City Council reviews FY 2026-27 capital spending priorities
items.summary: The council held a study session on the Capital Improvement Program for fiscal year 2026-27, which outlines major infrastructure and development projects the city plans to fund. This is worth monitoring if you care about what roads, parks, or facilities Mountain View will invest in over the next year.

items.headline: New tenant protections ordinance introduced for Sunnyvale
items.summary: The City Council introduced changes to the Residential Tenant Protections Program under municipal code Chapter 19.71. This ordinance could affect housing rights and landlord-tenant relationships for Sunnyvale residents.

items.headline: City releases Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan
items.summary: San José presented a status report on its Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan, outlining improvements to branch locations and services. This plan directly affects where residents can access library resources and what services will be available.

items.headline: Historic downtown building gets bonus floor space for upgrades
items.summary: The owner of 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto is requesting to elevate the building's historic status from Category 4 to Category 2, which would unlock 5,000 additional square feet of floor area in exchange for historic and seismic rehabilitation work.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A developer is proposing to rezone and redevelop two properties into an 8-story building with 167 residential units (28 affordable) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. This significant density increase will be reviewed through a quasi-judicial hearing process.

items.headline: City awards contract for 2026 local street pavement repairs
items.summary: The City Council approved bids and awarded a contract for the 2026 Local Streets Remove and Replace Pavement Project. This program will improve road conditions across San José's residential neighborhoods.

items.headline: City updates Active Transportation Plan with revised scoring criteria
items.summary: The City Council reviewed Phase 2 of the Active Transportation Plan, which shapes how Cupertino prioritizes walking and biking infrastructure. The plan includes new evaluation criteria that will determine which projects get built in your neighborhood.

items.headline: Council votes to change vacancy filling process for open seats
items.summary: Milpitas is amending how it handles City Council vacancies, potentially changing the procedure for appointing or electing replacements when council members leave office. The specific changes to the vacancy ordinance could affect how residents are represented if a seat opens up.
